name: Draggable Card
summary: An interactive 3D card component that responds to drag gestures with realistic physics-based motion, allowing users to grab, tilt, and manipulate cards in three-dimensional space.
detailedDescription: The Draggable Card component transforms static cards into interactive 3D objects that users can physically manipulate through drag gestures. This component creates an engaging, tactile experience where cards respond to mouse or touch drag with realistic physics-based motion. As users drag the card, it tilts and rotates in 3D space following the cursor position, creating a convincing illusion of holding a physical object. The effect uses CSS 3D transforms combined with spring physics for natural, weighty motion that feels responsive without being mechanical. Cards can be configured to return to center when released, stay in their dragged position, or trigger actions based on drag distance (like swipe-to-dismiss patterns). Visual feedback includes dynamic shadows that shift with card angle, enhancing the 3D illusion. Perfect for interactive portfolios, product showcases, card-based interfaces, or any context where adding physical, tactile interaction enhances engagement. The component supports both mouse and touch input, ensuring consistent behavior across desktop and mobile devices.
purpose: Create tactile, physical card interactions through drag-based manipulation
Engage users with responsive 3D motion that feels natural and weighty
Support swipe gestures for card dismissal or action triggering
Add memorable interactive moments to card-based interfaces
Bridge digital and physical through realistic physics simulation
behavior: Tracks mouse or touch drag gestures in real-time
Applies 3D rotateX and rotateY transforms based on drag position
Uses spring physics for natural, weighty motion response
Dynamically adjusts shadows to match card tilt angle
Supports configurable release behavior (return, stay, or dismiss)
Triggers actions based on drag distance thresholds
Works consistently across mouse and touch input methods
visualCharacteristics: Interactive 3D card responding to drag with realistic motion
Dynamic tilting and rotation following cursor position
Natural, physics-based movement feeling weighty and responsive
Tactile quality bridging digital and physical interaction
Engaging, playful aesthetic inviting user exploration
recommendations: Pairs well with testimonials, feature grids, and product highlight sections.
